messages.content:
Excellent! I've captured comprehensive details for T-Mobile's telecommunications network monitoring demo:

✅ **Company:** T-Mobile
✅ **Industry:** Telecommunications
✅ **Use Cases:** 4G/5G network monitoring with 5 detailed failure scenarios
✅ **Pain Points:** HSS failures, cell tower issues, security attacks, MME bugs, core network problems
✅ **Scale:** 500+ subscribers, 2-5 MME hosts, 50+ cell IDs
✅ **Metrics:** IMSI/Cell ID cardinality changes, spike detection, failure thresholds
✅ **Watcher Logic:** Fully defined with serial diff analysis

Your demo is **ready to generate**! I have everything needed to create sample data that will trigger your watcher across all 5 scenarios.

Type **'generate'** to create your demo configuration.

pain_points: Need to detect network issues before help desk is overwhelmed
HSS database corruption or synchronization issues affecting subscriber authentication
Radio equipment failure and cell tower handoff failures
MME software bugs and resource exhaustion
Rogue network attempts and SS7 security attacks
Core network split-brain and signaling storms
Dropped calls during mobility and interrupted data sessions
Service downtime leading to revenue loss and SLA violations
use_cases: Track unique IMSI and cell ID cardinality changes over time
Identify cell tower handoff cascade failures
Detect core network split-brain or signaling storms
Alert on sustained spike patterns across multiple lag periods
Detect security attacks or roaming misconfigurations
Detect mass authentication failure events (HSS issues)
Monitor 4G and 5G network for issues and proactively take action
Analyze UE Initiated Service Request procedure failures
Identify MME software bugs or resource exhaustion
context.scale: 500+ unique subscribers affected during incidents, 2-5 MME hosts monitored, 50+ unique cell IDs
metrics: Cell spike detection across lag periods
Percent change in IMSI and cell ID metrics
Procedure failure counts (minimum 200 failures per 5-minute interval)
IMSI spike detection across lag periods
Unique cell ID count changes (cell_id_change_1 > 50, cell_id_change_2 > 25)
